figures.caption: Figure 3: I/O space for Max under another specification requires a.Length > 1; ensures m >= 0. The blue region is ¬Pre, the pink region is Post, and their union is the SpecCheck region. The green region is GT (ground truth).

Figure 2: Seven-step spectest generation pipeline on a running Max example. Starting from a Dafny source file, the pipeline extracts a target method, constructs a runtime-checkable SpecCheck predicate, drops non-compilable cases, generates validated positive seeds, mutates them into spectests, assembles and runs the test file to obtain the completeness score (shown as neg_test_acc in Step 6), and finally enhances the suite in Step 7.
Table 3: Comparison with general-purpose LLMs on DafnyComp-Spec (%). SpecRL is trained only on Py2Dfy-Spec. We report Verifiable and Completeness to focus on final specification quality.

Table 1: Main results on DafnyComp-Spec (%). Bold marks the best value per model size. For Verifiable and Completeness, the second column in each pass@𝑘 pair reports the change relative to the SFT checkpoint. Spec-Superiority is a third-party auxiliary metric computed using ReForm’s implication-based criterion.
Figure 1: A Dafny method Max with specifications and auxiliary annotations. The verifier checks that every execution satisfying the precondition also satisfies all postconditions.
